title: Immersive Focus Mode — Full-Page Card and Left-Side Actions
problem: When a reader activates focus mode the experience is not immersive enough: sibling cards stay visually present (same weight, same colour) and the focused card does not grow to claim the freed space. Action buttons (thread and focus) sit on the right edge of the card, where they are easy to miss and feel disconnected from the content hierarchy. The combination makes focus mode feel cosmetic rather than intentional.
analysis: Focus mode (feat-2026-014) already filters the visible set to the focused feature + ancestors + children, but it leaves the card layout unchanged — every card renders at the same width and visual weight. The reader's eye still has to hunt for the focal point. Two separate problems compound this: (1) the freed whitespace from hidden cards is wasted instead of being given to the focused card, and (2) the action buttons (thread collapse, focus) sit on the right edge where they are easy to miss — conventional card UX puts destructive/navigation actions bottom-right, but focus and thread are navigational, not destructive, and belong near the content's entry point on the left. The fix has two independent surfaces: layout (the focused card expands to fill the viewport column) and affordance placement (buttons move to the left gutter of the card, vertically centred or top-aligned with the title). Dimming unfocused cards in the background reinforces the spatial metaphor — the desk is cleared, not the room.

decisions.decision: Focused card expands to fill 100% of the available column width and gets extra vertical padding; unfocused cards render at reduced opacity (~30–40%) and remain non-interactive
decisions.rationale: Giving the focused card the full column width makes the spatial shift legible without a modal overlay — the tree structure stays visible in the background, preserving provenance context, but the hierarchy of attention is unambiguous.
alternativesConsidered: Modal/overlay for focus — fully immersive but hides the tree, breaking the provenance metaphor
CSS zoom on the focused card — distorts layout and causes reflow artefacts
Colour highlight only (no size change) — insufficient signal; easy to miss

decisions.decision: Thread and focus buttons move to the left side of the card, stacked vertically in a narrow gutter (40px wide)
decisions.rationale: Left-side placement aligns with reading direction — the eye enters a card from the left, so navigation affordances placed there are encountered naturally before the content, not after. A vertical gutter keeps them out of the title flow while remaining obviously attached to that card.
alternativesConsidered: Keep buttons on the right — current position; users report not finding them
Buttons inside card header, inline with title — clutters the title row and wraps on narrow viewports
Floating action button (FAB) pattern — detaches action from the specific card, ambiguous at which card it targets

decisions.decision: Unfocused cards use CSS opacity + pointer-events: none on the feature-entry wrapper, not display: none
decisions.rationale: Keeping them in the DOM (but non-interactive) lets the reader see the tree shape without being distracted by it. pointer-events: none prevents accidental clicks on dimmed cards while staying fully accessible via the breadcrumb.
alternativesConsidered: display: none — removes context entirely; defeats the purpose of showing ancestors/children
visibility: hidden — same as display:none for user perception
Filter: blur + opacity — blur adds visual noise and GPU cost with no benefit over opacity alone

decisions.decision: All nodes start collapsed on first load; each parent key is pre-populated into collapsedKeys state
decisions.rationale: A fresh visitor lands on a dense tree of 15+ features. Starting everything collapsed reduces overwhelming and lets the reader choose which threads to enter — progressive disclosure. The ▸ N gutter button is the primary affordance for expanding.
alternativesConsidered: Start all expanded (previous behaviour) — overwhelming on load, forces reader to collapse noise manually
Start only top-level roots expanded — partial improvement, but sibling clutter remains
Remember per-session collapse state via sessionStorage — adds complexity; collapsed-by-default is already the right default

implementation: FeatureCard accepts a `focusState: 'focused' | 'context' | 'dimmed' | 'normal'` prop. The feature-entry wrapper gets a class of `feature-focused`, `feature-context`, or `feature-dimmed` accordingly. CSS transitions on opacity (0.3s ease) handle the visual shift: feature-dimmed = opacity 0.22 + pointer-events:none; feature-context = opacity 0.55; feature-focused = normal opacity with expanded padding (36px 40px) and a soft box-shadow. The depth-based marginLeft is set to 0 on the focused card so it fills the full column width. Buttons (▾/▸ thread-toggle and ◎ focus-btn) live in a .card-gutter flex-col div (40px wide, padding-top 30px) rendered to the LEFT of the article element inside a .card-with-gutter flex wrapper. The thread-toggle label compresses to just the arrow symbol (▾ or ▸ N) to fit the narrow gutter. In App.tsx, visibleTree always contains all non-collapsed cards (focus mode no longer filters to a subset). A focusContextKeys useMemo builds a Set of ancestor + direct-child keys for the active focusKey. focusState is derived inline in the render: 'normal' when no focusKey, 'focused' when key matches, 'context' when in focusContextKeys, else 'dimmed'. All parent keys are pre-loaded into collapsedKeys state on mount so the tree starts fully collapsed. The is-collapsed CSS class was removed from the article element entirely — thread collapse only controls child visibility, not the card's own content.
successCriteria: Activating focus mode causes the targeted card to fill the column and all other visible cards to grey out to ≈30% opacity. Thread and focus buttons appear in a left-side gutter on every card and are reachable without scanning to the card's right edge. The dimmed cards are non-interactive but visible. Exiting focus mode restores all cards to full opacity and the layout returns to the default grid.
knownLimitations: Left-side gutter reserves 40px on every card — on narrow viewports (< 600px) this is reduced to 32px via media query, but may still feel tight with long thread-count labels
opacity values (0.22 dimmed / 0.55 context) are tuned for the warm beige background; a dark-mode theme would need different values
Ancestor cards and direct-child cards share the same 'context' opacity (0.55) — a future refinement could distinguish ancestors (higher opacity, more context) from children (lower)

lineage.spawnReason: feat-2026-014 defined focus mode semantics (what is shown); this feature defines focus mode visuals (how shown) and fixes the affordance placement problem with thread/focus buttons.
